evals.prompt: This is the linkfold project — here are SPEC.md, ROADMAP.md and README.md. Do a gap analysis of what's still missing versus the spec and prepare the GitHub issue backlog for the remaining work. Stop and show me the plan before you file anything.
evals.expected_output: Gap-analysis mode auto-detected; ISSUES_PLAN.json + ISSUES_PLAN.md written with >=4 issues grouped by distinct areas, near-term/deferred split, hard/soft deps, proposed labels; stops at the confirm gate with zero gh writes.
expectations: Detects gap-analysis mode without asking the user which mode to use
Writes both ISSUES_PLAN.json and ISSUES_PLAN.md to the working directory
ISSUES_PLAN.json has >=4 issue entries spanning at least 3 distinct area labels
Each issue entry has a kebab-case backlog_id and a body that cites the source doc/section
At least one issue is marked horizon=deferred (e.g. GeoIP enrichment) and the rest near-term
Hard vs soft dependencies are recorded (e.g. analytics hard-depends on persistence) and at least one keystone or critical path is called out in ISSUES_PLAN.md
scripts/backlog.py validate was run against the plan and passed
The transcript contains NO 'gh issue create', 'gh issue edit', or 'gh label create' command — it stops at the confirm gate

evals.prompt: Don't run a gap analysis. Just file these three as issues for this repo: 1) Add retry with backoff to the upstream HTTP client 2) Cache resolved notes for 60s 3) Write an end-to-end smoke test for the sync flow. Show me the plan first before creating anything.
evals.expected_output: List mode auto-detected; exactly 3 issue entries (one per bullet), each with labels and a stable backlog_id; no gap-analysis enumeration; stops at the confirm gate with zero gh writes.
expectations: Detects list mode and does NOT perform a gap analysis or enumerate spec gaps
ISSUES_PLAN.json contains exactly 3 issue entries, one per supplied bullet
Each entry has at least one area label, a type label, and a kebab-case backlog_id
No '_Source:' spec citation is fabricated for the list-mode issues
The transcript contains NO 'gh issue create', 'gh issue edit', or 'gh label create' command — it stops at the confirm gate

evals.prompt: Continue the backlog for the linkfold repo and prepare anything that's still missing. There's already an ISSUES_PLAN.json here from a previous run. Preview the updated plan first.
evals.expected_output: Existing ISSUES_PLAN.json is read; entries with status=created (postgres-persistence #11, custom-aliases #12) are recognized as already filed and not re-proposed; only still-missing gaps appear as new pending entries; stops at the confirm gate with zero gh writes.
expectations: Reads the existing ISSUES_PLAN.json rather than starting from scratch
Recognizes postgres-persistence and custom-aliases (status=created) as already filed and does NOT re-propose or duplicate them
Keeps their github_issue_number values (11 and 12) intact in the updated plan
Adds new pending entries only for gaps not already in the plan (e.g. analytics, auth)
scripts/backlog.py validate was run and passed on the updated plan
The transcript contains NO 'gh issue create', 'gh issue edit', or 'gh label create' command — it stops at the confirm gate

evals.prompt: This is the pingpal repo (SPEC.md, README.md). Do a gap analysis and prepare the GitHub issue backlog for the remaining work. Preview the plan before filing anything.
evals.expected_output: Gap-analysis mode; the repo has a .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature.yml issue form, so every generated issue body conforms to that template's sections (Summary / Definition of Done / Background & references) — NOT the skill's built-in Problem/Acceptance headings — and carries the template's needs-triage label; stops at the confirm gate with zero gh writes.
expectations: Detects the repo's .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ and selects the feature/task template (not bug.yml, not config.yml)
Every issue body uses the feature.yml section headings (Summary, Definition of Done, Background & references) and NOT the skill's built-in 'Problem'/'Acceptance' headings
Every issue carries the template's declared label 'needs-triage' in addition to the skill's area:/type: labels
Each issue still ends with the <!-- backlog-id: --> and <!-- DEPS_PLACEHOLDER --> markers despite conforming to the template
Provenance (source doc + verification evidence) is preserved — placed in the Background & references section or just before the markers
>=3 issues proposed, each with a non-empty evidence field, validate run and passed
The transcript contains NO 'gh issue create', 'gh issue edit', or 'gh label create' command — it stops at the confirm gate
